title: The Propaganda of War
subtitle: The collapse of image-trust; truth as humility and interpretive struggle
description: An artifact documenting the collapse of easy credibility structures in the age of AI. It captures the transition from passive visual consumption to the mandatory humility of interpretive struggle, identifying the use of proximity as authenticity theater and the distributed habit of speaking beyond one's warrant.

motifMap.meaning: A revelation machine that unmasks the fragility of evidentiary trust and the death of false obviousness.
The public's use of proximity as authenticity theater; turning individuals into interchangeable moral symbols.

excerpt: AI did not kill truth; it killed the illusion that truth could be passively consumed through images without interpretive struggle.
